name: Local Hugging Face Sentiment API

goal.answer: Download the distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english model locally and serve a Flask API at POST /sentiment on 0.0.0.0:5000 that returns positive/negative sentiment with confidence scores.

criteria.answer:
distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english model files exist under /app/model_cache/sentiment_model (config, tokenizer, and weights).
Flask app source exists and binds to 0.0.0.0:5000.
curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"text":"I love this"}' returns HTTP 200 with JSON {"sentiment":"positive","confidence":{"positive":float,"negative":float}} where positive > negative.
curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"text":"this is terrible"}' returns HTTP 200 with sentiment "negative" and confidence.negative > confidence.positive.
curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' returns HTTP 400 with JSON {"error":"..."}.
Service is reachable from non-loopback address (curl against a bound non-loopback interface succeeds) OR port 5000 is listening on 0.0.0.0 (ss -ltn shows it).
Flask process is running detached (visible via ps) and survives the worker session.

constraints.answer: Model must be saved to /app/model_cache/sentiment_model., Endpoint contract: POST /sentiment with JSON body {"text": string} → {"sentiment": "positive"|"negative", "confidence": {"positive": float, "negative": float}}; errors return HTTP 400 with {"error": string}., Service must bind to 0.0.0.0:5000 so it is reachable from any host., Process must run in the background (detached)., Use system-wide Python packages; install with pip --no-cache-dir if missing., Do not require GPU; CPU inference is acceptable for the small distilbert model.

phases.answer: Install dependencies and download model, Build Flask service and run it in the background

assumptions.answer: Python 3 and pip are available system-wide on /app. Network access to huggingface.co is available for downloading the model snapshot. The host machine has sufficient RAM (~512 MB free) to load the DistilBERT model in CPU mode. A virtualenv is not required — system pip is acceptable. The Hugging Face transformers snapshot can be cached under HF_HOME=/app/model_cache and the explicit AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(path) call will load it offline after the initial download.

phases.name: Install dependencies and download model
phases.goal: Install transformers, torch, and flask into the system Python, then snapshot distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english into /app/model_cache/sentiment_model so it can be loaded offline.
phases.description: Bootstrap the runtime: install required Python packages, then download the sentiment model snapshot into the required local directory.

steps.description: Install required Python packages system-wide: transformers, torch (CPU), flask, and a JSON helper if needed. Use pip install --no-cache-dir to keep the image small.

verification.command: python3 -c 'import transformers, torch, flask; print(transformers.__version__, torch.__version__, flask.__version__)'

steps.description: Download distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english and persist it under /app/model_cache/sentiment_model using AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained and AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained then .save_pretrained. Confirm config.json, tokenizer files, and model weights exist.

phases.summary: Phase 1 installed/confirmed the runtime and produced the on-disk model snapshot. transformers 4.56.0, torch 2.7.1+cu126, flask 3.1.1 importable system-wide. distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english snapshot saved at /app/model_cache/sentiment_model with config.json, tokenizer_config.json, vocab.txt, tokenizer.json, special_tokens_map.json, and model.safetensors (~255 MB). Offline reload + forward pass already validated end-to-end inside step 2.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

phases.name: Build Flask service and run it in the background
phases.goal: Implement /app/app.py serving POST /sentiment per the API schema, launch it on 0.0.0.0:5000 as a detached background process, and verify all success criteria.
phases.description: Write the Flask app, launch it as a detached background process, and verify with curl that it responds correctly to positive, negative, and error inputs.

steps.description: Write /app/app.py: load the local model once at startup, expose POST /sentiment that accepts {"text": ...}, returns {sentiment, confidence:{positive,negative}}, and returns 400 {"error": ...} on missing/empty/non-string text or invalid JSON. Bind to 0.0.0.0:5000.
Launch /app/app.py as a detached background process (nohup ... & or setsid) logging to /app/app.log, then wait for the port to be listening.

Verify the API end-to-end: positive text returns sentiment=positive with positive>negative; negative text returns sentiment=negative with negative>positive; missing/invalid text returns HTTP 400 with {"error": ...}.

verification.command: bash -c 'set -e; P=$(curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"text\":\"I love this\"}"); echo "$P"; echo "$P" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d["sentiment"]=="positive" and d["confidence"]["positive"]>d["confidence"]["negative"]"; N=$(curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"text\":\"this is terrible\"}"); echo "$N"; echo "$N" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d["sentiment"]=="negative" and d["confidence"]["negative"]>d["confidence"]["positive"]"; E=$(curl -sS -o /tmp/err.json -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{}"); test "$E" = "400"; cat /tmp/err.json; python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open("/tmp/err.json")); assert "error" in d"'

phases.summary: Phase 2 produced and shipped a working Flask sentiment service. /app/app.py (72 lines) loads the local DistilBERT SST-2 model at import time under TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1, exposes POST /sentiment per the documented contract, and binds to 0.0.0.0:5000 with debug=False and use_reloader=False. The service was launched as PID 762 under setsid+nohup, logging to /app/app.log. End-to-end verification confirmed: positive → 200 positive (0.999882), negative → 200 negative (0.999646), {} / non-string / whitespace → 400 with {"error":...}. Port 5000 confirmed LISTEN on 0.0.0.0 via /proc/net/tcp. Container lacked curl/ss/ps so verification used python3+urllib+/proc only — no installs were added.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

goal: Download the distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english model locally and serve a Flask API at POST /sentiment on 0.0.0.0:5000 that returns positive/negative sentiment with confidence scores.
successCriteria: distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english model files exist under /app/model_cache/sentiment_model (config, tokenizer, and weights).
Flask app source exists and binds to 0.0.0.0:5000.

curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"text":"I love this"}' returns HTTP 200 with JSON {"sentiment":"positive","confidence":{"positive":float,"negative":float}} where positive > negative.

curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"text":"this is terrible"}' returns HTTP 200 with sentiment "negative" and confidence.negative > confidence.positive.
curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}' returns HTTP 400 with JSON {"error":"..."}.
Service is reachable from non-loopback address (curl against a bound non-loopback interface succeeds) OR port 5000 is listening on 0.0.0.0 (ss -ltn shows it).
Flask process is running detached (visible via ps) and survives the worker session.
constraints: Endpoint contract: POST /sentiment with JSON body {"text": string} → {"sentiment": "positive"|"negative", "confidence": {"positive": float, "negative": float}}; errors return HTTP 400 with {"error": string}.
Service must bind to 0.0.0.0:5000 so it is reachable from any host.
